title: Debug Service Logging Flow
description: Initialization and structured log capture/retrieval via DebugService

steps: register('debug', new DebugService()) → adds to service registry
Service framework calls _init() on all registered services
Register param provider callback on svc_exec
Call puter.logger.on(category) → enables log category in global Puter logger
[For each iframe] call iframe.contentWindow.postMessage(msg) → broadcast enable-log to child frame

key_relationships: DebugService extends Service (base class from definitions.js)
DebugService depends_on ExecService (retrieves via service locator: services.get('exec'))
DebugService calls puter.logger.on (global puter SDK logger)
DebugService broadcasts postMessage to all DOM iframes via jQuery
ExecService uses DebugService param provider to inject enabled_logs as query param
data_flow: enabled_logs[] → ExecService param provider → query param 'enabled_logs' (semicolon-separated)
category → postMessage payload → all child iframes
important_notes: DebugService is a side-effect-only service: it maintains a list of enabled log categories, propagates them to ExecService (for process-launch query params) and to all iframes (via postMessage). No return values. puter.logger is a global object from the Puter SDK. jQuery $('iframe').each iterates all iframes in the current page context.
